notes: First the game crashed with a popup textbox, but still appeared running in steam. I had to properly terminate it with SIGTERM. Then I disabled D3D11 with PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 as mentioned in previous reviews and the game launched. Controller seems completely broken even in the menus so I played with the mouse/keyboard. At some point the mouse also became broken (I don't remember if an alt-tabbing was involved) and since the game do not save between chapters, the trick for me was to get out of fullscreen mode to windowed mode to avoid restarting, then the mouse was working again. Otherwise performance was OK given my hardware. I have to note that I was using an optimus laptop with an NVIDIA GTX 750M GPU and the nvidia driver, version 415.25 (steam does not seem to detect it). My full command in steam launch options was : PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 primusrun %command%
title: Dear Esther: Landmark Edition

notes:
First the game crashed with a popup textbox, but still appeared running in steam. I had to properly terminate it with SIGTERM. Then I disabled D3D11 with PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 as mentioned in previous reviews and the game launched. Controller seems completely broken even in the menus so I played with the mouse/keyboard. At some point the mouse also became broken (I don't remember if an alt-tabbing was involved) and since the game do not save between chapters, the trick for me was to get out of fullscreen mode to windowed mode to avoid restarting, then the mouse was working again. Finally the cave level had big issues when moving around. The character speed repeatedly went up to the point where it was almost unplayable and I could almost fly over the level. I am not completely sure those bugs are wine related though. Weird that the last one occurs only in the caves (even after restarting the game), but it did not prevent me from completing the level and the whole game.
Otherwise performance was OK given my hardware. I have to note that I was using an optimus laptop with an NVIDIA GTX 750M GPU and the nvidia driver, version 415.25 (steam does not seem to detect it). My full command in steam launch options was : PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 primusrun %command%
title: Dear Esther: Landmark Edition

notes: runs better than the original, great out of the box
title: Dear Esther: Landmark Edition
